company.name: Venturian AI
company.tagline: Earn weekly with the future of AI.

employees.name: Harry Chen
employees.title: Founder & CEO
employees.bio: Built Venturian to help students earn real weekly income while contributing to AI training pipelines. Former student himself — started this because no legitimate option existed.

employees.name: Priya Nambiar
employees.title: VP of Engineering
employees.bio: Distributed systems engineer. Built core infra at Databricks before joining as employee #4.

employees.name: James Okafor
employees.title: Staff Research Scientist
employees.bio: PhD in computational linguistics from CMU. Focus on evaluation frameworks and model robustness.

employees.name: Camille Renard
employees.title: Head of Product
employees.bio: Former PM at Linear and Vercel. Brings a developer-tools lens to every product decision.

employees.name: Tariq Essawi
employees.title: Senior Software Engineer
employees.bio: Full-stack with a backend lean. Owns the API layer and developer SDK.

employees.name: Yuki Tanaka
employees.title: Lead Designer
employees.bio: Previously at Figma. Believes that design systems are the highest-leverage design work.

employees.name: Amara Diallo
employees.title: Research Engineer
employees.bio: Masters from Stanford AI Lab. Bridges research and production, turning papers into shipped features.

employees.name: Devon Park
employees.title: Enterprise Account Executive
employees.bio: Focused on financial services and healthcare verticals. Loves the consultative, technical sale.

reviews.title: One of the best engineering cultures I've been part of
reviews.body: The bar for quality is genuinely high — code reviews are thorough, documentation is expected, and nobody ships something they'd be embarrassed to explain. It's demanding but the output reflects it.
reviews.pros: Smart colleagues, real technical depth, strong async culture
reviews.cons: Fast pace can make it hard to go deep on any one problem

reviews.title: Genuinely mission-driven, not just in the deck
reviews.body: Leadership talks about responsible AI constantly and backs it up with actual prioritisation decisions, including ones that cost short-term revenue. Refreshing to see that at a startup.
reviews.pros: Clear mission, thoughtful leadership, good research autonomy
reviews.cons: Tooling for researchers is still catching up to the pace of hiring

reviews.title: Product team has real influence here
reviews.body: PMs aren't order-takers. I've pushed back on roadmap items, killed features, and redirected engineering time — and leadership supported it when the reasoning was solid. Rare.
reviews.pros: High ownership, collaborative eng team, fast iteration cycles
reviews.cons: Design resources stretched thin across too many surfaces

reviews.title: Steep learning curve, then very rewarding
reviews.body: The first two months are overwhelming — the domain is hard, the codebase is large, and expectations are high. Once you're ramped, the work is genuinely interesting and your contributions matter.
reviews.cons: Onboarding process needs more structure for new grads

reviews.title: Great product, growing pains in operations
reviews.body: The technical work is excellent and the vision is clear. But as the company scales, some of the operational fundamentals — performance reviews, career ladders, cross-team coordination — haven't kept up.
reviews.pros: Strong product to sell, good market timing
reviews.cons: Sales process and internal tooling feel underinvested

reviews.title: The design culture punches above its size
reviews.body: For a 90-person company, the design system maturity and the level of craft expected is more like a 500-person org. Everyone from eng to leadership has a strong visual intuition, which makes collaboration unusually smooth.
reviews.pros: Design is a first-class function, not an afterthought
reviews.cons: User research capacity is limited given the product surface area
